scenarios.description: Stop the application container during repeated catalog probes and measure recovery after restart.
scenarios.gracefulDegradation: No graceful degradation observed; requests failed until the app container restarted.
scenarios.recoveryBehavior: Recovered after dependency restoration without manual workaround.

scenarios.description: Stop PostgreSQL while repeatedly attempting customer login; then restore the database and observe recovery.
scenarios.gracefulDegradation: The API surfaced backend errors while PostgreSQL was unavailable.
scenarios.recoveryBehavior: Recovered after dependency restoration without manual workaround.

scenarios.description: Stop Redis during checkout so payment-order persistence fails, then restore Redis and retry.
scenarios.gracefulDegradation: Checkout failed fast when Redis-backed payment-order persistence was unavailable.
scenarios.recoveryBehavior: Recovered after dependency restoration without manual workaround.

scenarios.module: Authentication and authorization / Product catalog and listing
scenarios.description: Run a local proxy that adds 1200ms latency before forwarding login and catalog requests.
scenarios.gracefulDegradation: Availability remained intact, but latency increased materially because requests were delayed before reaching the API.
scenarios.recoveryBehavior: Recovery was immediate once the latency proxy was removed.

recommendations: app_downtime_catalog: consider graceful maintenance responses or health-gated routing in front of the app container.
redis_checkout_failure: checkout should expose clearer dependency-failure diagnostics and possibly queue/retry payment-order persistence.
injected_latency_proxy: client-visible latency rises directly; consider timeout budgets and user-facing retry guidance.
